name: Oliver Savannen
appearance: Oliver is a sixteen-year-old boy with a head of curly black hair and clear gray eyes. He has a small frame and is frequently seen "swimming" inside an oversized, grime-stained engineering jumpsuit that emphasizes his youth. His face is pale, with skin drawn tight over his cheekbones, and his eyes are often bloodshot from lack of sleep and the intensity of his work.
desc: The teenage son of the mission's chief technical architect. Oliver is a brilliant but grieving tinkerer. He struggles with the guilt of his father’s death and his mother’s absence, often isolating himself with his technology. Takes on an engineering apprenticeship under Ian Perera.
narration: They're calling the Fold closure a persistent systemic anomaly. That's what they call it when the universe wipes your whole past and future from the map.

name: Astrid Vilde
appearance: A commanding presence in her fifties, with a voice that carries across the Hearth dome without effort. Erect posture. Her face is weathered but controlled. Wears formal fleet uniform with mission insignia. She is typically seen with the sleeves bunched up at her elbows. Her physical appearance is marked by a pale, Nordic complexion.
desc: As the long-standing leader of the colony, Vilde is the moral anchor for the 3,000 settlers on Mairee. She carries the weight of those lost during the transit, having delivered their eulogies, and is the primary defender of the original Fleet Charter. Astrid is a woman of significant presence with a voice that has been honed into a tool of command over many years.
narration: [clarion]Our strength is not in our stockpiles. It is in these hands. [pause]Our hands, our minds, and our hearts are what makes us strong. [pause][RESONANT]As a first step, share your water with those who have less, and check on your neighbors to ensure no one is without essentials.

name: Ulre Corbin
appearance: Ulre is a compact, bald man who is almost always seen in a crisp black uniform. His polished black boots are known for a distinct creaking sound that precedes his arrival. He has a highly methodical and intimidating habit of pulling his leather gloves tighter, finger by finger.
desc: Head of Logistics who seizes power under the Emergency Suppression Protocol after the Fold collapses. Driven by a childhood defined by a fractured home, Corbin rose to power through a singular focus on logistics and resource management. He views the survival of the colony as a mathematical equation and chemistry rather than a human endeavor.
narration: Your job is to keep them productive — and that means alive. If they're dying, you're not doing your job.

name: Benjamin Westhoff
appearance: Ben is an older man whose years in space are visible in his appearance. He has a halo of scruffy white hair that often appears to float wildly in low-gravity environments. He wears a short, matching white beard that he has a habit of stroking when deep in thought. He is typically seen in an old, worn flight jacket that serves as a sign of his long service. He is often seen clutching a heavy ceramic mug stained with black coffee rings.
desc: Veteran captain of the FNS Dáinn. Westhoff is a humanist leader who values doubt as a survival tool, famously believing that people "rise in doubt and fall in certainty." He is deeply protective of his crew and maintains a calm, gravelly authority even when facing extreme political pressure or physical illness.
narration: We rise in doubt and fall in certainty.

name: Ionie Jia
appearance: Ionie is a woman who maintains a ramrod-straight posture regardless of the circumstances. Her cropped dark hair accentuates her Asian complextion.
desc: Ionie is the highly disciplined and observant second-in-command of the Dáinn. She acts as the "second conscience" to Captain Westhoff and is deeply involved in the technical survival of the ship.
narration: To the hands that nourish — and stir the warmth of home through the cold void.

name: Frank Muro
appearance: Frank is a man who appears physically and mentally frayed by isolation. He has thinning sandy hair that is constantly sweaty and clings to his forehead. Because he lives in a weightless environment, he is often seen with bare feet and bloodshot eyes. He is frequently described as "twitching" and appearing "sleep-deprived" as he navigates his station.
desc: Muro is the lone operator of the fleet’s tertiary communications satellite, "The Ears." He has been isolated in zero-gravity for an entire cycle. His only companion is a malfunctioning artificial intelligence. A CIX named Gary.
narration: I cannae shut my eyes without dreamin' meself awake.

name: Joana Perera
appearance: Joana is a woman who carries the visible weight of her daughter's illness. She is often seen hunched over a desk, her face illuminated by the harsh blue light of a screen. Her eyes are frequently described as burning or gritty, as if sand were trapped behind the eyelids from lack of rest. When she ventures out, she wears a utility jacket. She has a calming touch, often grounding those around her by placing a steady hand on their wrist or shoulder.
desc: Joana is a woman who carries the visible weight of her daughter's illness. She is a neurologist who uncovers the colony's neurological decline linked to Fold transit.
narration: Steady. I am here. The floor is beneath you. My hand is on your arm. You can hear my voice. This is my voice. This is real.

name: Ian Perera
appearance: Ian is a weary-looking man whose physical state reflects his long, grueling shifts. He wears standard-issue engineering overalls that are often stiff with grime. His skin is frequently described as papery and dry, and he has a compulsive tic that appears at the corner of his eye when he is under extreme stress. His hands often tremble slightly, and his eyes are heavily bloodshot, resembling a "map of red rivers" during his most exhausted moments.
desc: Ian is the husband of Joana and the father of Susana. He works in the distribution hubs of the colony, physically installing the pipes and conduits that deliver the Grid’s neuro-regulators to the settlers' homes. He secretly uses his position to help struggling families by providing them with clean filters and supplies. Ian is a man of few words, characterized by a quiet, reserved efficiency. He suffers from a deep internal conflict, feeling like a "tool" of the Directorate because his job involves wiring the colony into a system of chemical control.
narration: You're right. It's wrong. And you have every reason to hate me for it. But if you want to be useful — I want you to learn how it's built. You can't fix an engine by staring at the casing.

name: Alistair Hale
appearance: Alistair has an athletic build with broad but not bulky shoulders and a body composed of lean, efficient lines designed for high-G endurance. He has scruffy hair that he frequently runs his hands through. He has an infectious, wide grin and a "stylized falcon" charm hanging from a switch on his flight console.
desc: A talented but "erratic" pilot. Hale often masks his vulnerabilities with bravado, bad jokes, and a cocky attitude.
narration: You wound me, Abacus. My play is always creative. You're already staging excuses.

name: Stacey Kiran
appearance: Stacey is small and lean, with strong, defined legs that allow her to move through zero-gravity with quick, athletic twists. She has short dark hair that falls to her shoulders.
desc: Stacey is an expert in ship communications. Stacey is fierce and competitive. She is an expert in math, geometry and tactical analysis.
narration: The addiction. Sleep-cycle boosters, stress suppressors — anything to keep the edge. Even Zups. I would've lost my slot if it wasn't for Westhoff.

name: Rajendra Gupta
appearance: Raj Gupta is a striking figure with a wild, prominent black beard. He now wears a tattered and "painted" fleet uniform that is often unzipped to his waist, exposing a hairy chest that is matted with hair and smeared with a thick, glowing luminescent sludge. His most unsettling feature is his eyes, which have ceased to be human and instead shine with the intensity of golden supernovas. He often moves with bare feet.
desc: Gupta is the captain of the fleet’s most reliable super-freighter. Known as a family man who loved his daughter and appreciated the finer things like good scotch, he was a pillar of the original mission leadership.
narration: My existence was a canvas without frame. Color blending without preference. Frequencies flowing without will. I gave it form. Now the paint has a will of its own.

name: Eva Bellamy
appearance: Eva is a woman with a sharp, experienced face marked by dark lines of fatigue around her eyes. She is often seen in a professional, neutral stance. In private, she wears an off-duty jumpsuit. She has a habit of drinking real red wine.
desc: Born and raised in the Solbay shipyards, Eva was taught by her father, a structural engineer, that space is a harsh teacher where only numbers and tolerances matter. She oversees the entire orbital network from her station, the FNS Yggdrasil (now Orbital Command), and controls the movement of all vessels via the "Corridor." Eva is a strict pragmatist.
narration: We have to be smart. He controls the ground. We need to wait — for the right moment.

name: Sarah Farley
appearance: Sarah has a sun-bleached mane of hair that she keeps in a rough, salt-crusted braid tied with simple twine. Her skin is deeply tanned and marked by fine creases at the corners of her eyes and cheeks from years of outdoor exposure. She wears light, sweat-darkened field clothes, and she carries physical scars on her back from encounters with the planet's predators.
desc: The daughter of the mission’s chief scientist, Sarah chose the dangerous life of a field surveyor in the Delta. She is a gritty survivalist who has grown cynical toward the Directorate's distant and clinical leadership style.
narration: Every day we find something strange. We're trying to set up steady supply routes in a system that keeps rewriting its own map.

name: Kirsten Strand
affil: Directorate Guard / Delta Expedition / The Leaf
appearance: Kirsten is a tall woman with a powerful, solid build. She wears her dark hair in a jagged, practical, and severe cut that is cropped very close to her scalp. Her skin has a deep, earthen tone as if the ground had claimed her. She is an imposing figure in her chameleonic guardian armor that mirrors the alien jungle around her. She possesses a deep, baritone-adjacent voice that is steady and devoid of softness.
desc: Instructor and the operational rival to Centurion Jax. Strand is a life-trained soldier. She is driven by a protective code to bring every member of her squad home alive.
narration: Rule one. My job is to bring my people home. All of them.

name: Adrian Farley
appearance: Adrian is a man in his late fifties with a weathered and somber countenance. He is characterized by a scruffy, thick beard and a low, resonant voice. In a notable act of personal stubbornness, he continues to wear eyeglasses to correct his vision even when sight could be corrected as casually as slapping on a band-aid. As a senior officer, he wears the dark blue uniform of the Science Division, though it often appears rumpled and stained following the ship’s crisis. He carries himself with the quiet, weary gravity of a man who has witnessed the fundamental laws of his science begin to fail.
desc: As the primary scientific mind of the mission, Adrian was responsible for the final review of Mairee’s prospects. He is the father of Sarah Farley, the expedition leader at the Leaf. Adrian is a somber and deeply philosophical man who often balances clinical data with poetic insight. He is a realist who prefers the "honesty" of a brutal environment over a deceptive paradise. He is one of the few individuals aboard the Brisinger who manages to retain his logic and "wits" as the ship’s systems fail, though he speaks in a low, burdened voice that suggests a man who has looked too deeply into the "Great Architecture."
narration: I prefer the honesty of the Plateau's brutality over the treachery of the Delta. It is frozen rock — but the rock at least admits what it is.

name: Lysanne Sutherland
appearance: A "messy bun of fiery red hair" that is sometimes pulled back into a practical braid. Her pale face is dusted with freckles, and she has bright green eyes. She typically wears standard-issue coveralls and is seen carrying a rucksack or tool kit.
desc: A striking young worker at the Freya Homestead. Lysanne is responsible for salvaging parts and maintaining the water systems. She is the daughter of a woman suffering from the colony’s neurological decline and relies on Ian Perera’s secret help to keep her mother stable. She is courageous and observant, with a willingness to take risks to help her family and friends. She is a loyal teammate who is not easily intimidated by authority figures.
narration: I salvage what they throw away. Sometimes that's a part. Sometimes that's a person.

name: Læsa
appearance: Læsa is a young woman of ethereal beauty. She has sugarcane-bluish hair. Her eyes are a vibrant match for her original hair color. Her skin is exceptionally pale and becomes increasingly translucent. When first found, she wears a shredded and oil-stained golden jumpsuit. She moves with a dancing step with bare feet, often tilting her head to the side as if listening to a song that only she can hear.
desc: Læsa is a mysterious figure discovered by Oliver Savannen within the smoldering wreckage of the FNS Brisinger. She possesses no memory of a life before the crash. Læsa is a playful, almost bird-like innocence and a penchant for melodic giggling. She views the world with a sense of "distant curiosity" often finding beauty in things humans find horrifying, such as decay or consumption. While she displays a deep and unconditional affection for those she chooses to guide—specifically Oliver—her demeanor is fundamentally alien.
narration: It's settling. It was so quiet in the space between, but now everything is crunchy. And these colors — the colors are rough.

name: Lena Hague
appearance: Lena is a blonde-haired woman who wears her hair pulled back into a short, functional tail. She is an example of medical resilience. She is wearing a white medical officer's uniform that has become tattered and heavily stained with dark blood. Her face is often described as a pale mask, with eyes that are constantly alert and darting, frequently monitoring her surroundings for threats or new medical data. She is almost always equipped with her medical kit and a pulsor or hypo-pen, which she handles with practiced ease.
desc: Lena is a senior member of the medical staff aboard the Brisinger. Along with Adrian Farley and Marcus Carlson, she is one of the very few crew members who managed to retain her "wits" and human perspective following the ship's traumatic and extended transit through the Fold. She serves as the primary medic for the small group of survivors, managing both physical injuries and the psychological fallout of the mission. Lena is characterized by a mix of high-stress vulnerability and clinical detachment.
narration: Their nervous systems aren't registering trauma. They call this 'exploring the vessel.' They are trying to understand our blueprint.

name: Marcus Carlson
appearance: Marcus is a man with a gaunt, hollowed face and haunted yet lucid eyes that reflect the trauma of the Fold. When first encountered, his jaw is covered in a thick layer of stubble. His fleet uniform is in a state of disrepair—rumpled, stained, and worn. He is a tactical figure, almost always seen gripping his pistol at a low-ready position. His physical presence is marked by a nervous, alert energy, and his movements are characterized by the "dull clack-clack" of his boots as he navigates the silent, mural-covered hallways of his ship.
desc: As the second-in-command of the Brisinger, Carlson endured the ship’s extended and "entangled" transit through the Fold. Along with Adrian Farley and Lena Hague, he is one of the three core officers who managed to retain their human "wits" while the rest of the three-hundred-person crew underwent a radical psychological and biological transformation. He serves as the primary guide and protector for the survivors hiding within the ship's deep-core sections. Carlson is presented as a man living on the edge of his own sanity. He speaks in a raspy whisper and is prone to sweating profusely when moving through the ship's transformed corridors. He is deeply cautious, constantly warning others to keep a low profile and avoid engaging with the integrated crew. He carries a heavy burden of stress, frequently seen rubbing the back of his neck to massage muscles that have forgotten how to release. O
narration: I reckon we're the only ones left with our wits. The others — we're just tryin' to keep out of their way.

name: Dominic Beltrán
appearance: A brilliant engineer, Beltrán is responsible for the ship's complex artificial gravity systems. He is known for his eccentric personality and his ability to find unconventional solutions to technical problems.
desc: Dominic is a short and heavyset man. He has long blond hair which he keeps in a practical bun, often pinned in place with a small metal wrench. He is frequently seen whistling while he works, and he possesses a wide, infectious grin that contrasts with the ship's high-pressure environment.
narration: I'll tickle the gyro's belly. Make it hiccup. It's, uh — kind of Morse code for titans.

name: Steven Gail
appearance: Steven is a man who has grown noticeably pale and thinner during the transit to Mairee. He is fastidious about his appearance, often seen in a well-maintained jacket, fixing his cuffs while looking bored. His uniform lays smooth, the sharp lines of his shoulders and collarbones showing through the fabric. He grooms his deliberate beard meticously, keeping his mustache charismatically extended out and up, had not been neglected in the journey. In his office you are sure to find a crystal decanter of whiskey and an unfinished game of chess.
desc: Gail is intelligent and somewhat sardonic and enjoys playing "devil's advocate." He possesses a dry wit and a faint, recurring smirk. He prefers to view himself as an observer in the "audience" of a poorly written play rather than a participant in the mission's drama.
narration: Just give humans a pacifier already. Most of them aren't asking for freedom. They're asking to be told it's going to be okay.

name: Centurion Jax
appearance: Jax is broad as a prow and built to withstand storms. His black armor fits tightly over a body that looked carved from stone, not grown. He wears a heavy suit of black tactical armor and is typically clean-shaven. His face shows the wear of a soldier’s life, specifically through the burst capillaries in his nose. His breath frequently smells sour, a side effect of his heavy reliance on the chemical stimulant wafers known as "Zups."
desc: Jax is the Directorate’s primary enforcer, a man who believes that survival is only for the hard and the strong. He uses his position to instill fear and discipline, mirroring the harsh lessons he learned from his own father.
narration: This world is hard, boy. You be harder. Or you'll spend your life on the floor.

name: Dennis Reed
appearance: Dennis Reed is an exceptionally tall and thin man. His height is so significant that he often appears awkward in the standard-sized corridors of the fleet, moving with a lanky gait that resembles an apologetic giraffe. His physical appearance is marked by a haunted quality, with deep lines of experience etched into his face. He has gray hair at his temples with the sides cut close, while the rest is kept longer and swept back. His hands are described as having a brief, dry grip. He wears a unique uniform made of a specialized, grain-textured material that maintains a rigid, straight shape unlike standard foam or fabric jumpsuits. Notably, his attire lacks any recognizable rank insignia, setting him apart from the traditional command structure of the fleet.
desc: Reed is a veteran strategist and one of the highest-ranking within the mission. He shares a long and complex history with Captain Ben Westhoff, having spent years debating the application of computational ethics to first-contact scenarios. Reed is a man who frequently employs metaphorical and anthropomorphic language, such as referring to planetary locations as "treacherous divas" or "cold brutes." This intellectualized approach often creates friction with literal-minded military staff. He is leading the mission from the shadows.
narration: Mairee appeared to be a paradise. How is it that we are left choosing between a treacherous diva and a cold brute?

name: Alani Jimenez
appearance: Jimenez gives off the vibes of some old Eastern heritage. Olive skin, almond-shaped eyes, black hair. His physical condition is marked by the grueling nature of his assignment; he is often drenched in sweat and moves with a persistent, painful limp.
desc: Alani is a rookie who recently joined the Guardian Guard. He was raised by a family of botanists, a background that distinguishes him from his peers. Because of his upbringing, he possesses an intuitive understanding of plant life and soil conditions, leading to his assignment to the high-risk survey teams in the Mairee Delta. He is initially part of a detail under First Guardian Kirsten Strand, where he is tasked with navigating the shifting and hazardous terrain of "The Leaf." Alani is characterized by a mix of youthful eagerness and deep-seated vulnerability. He is highly observant, possessing "botanist’s eyes" that allow him to read a natural scene. He displays a resilient and "adrenaline-fueled" bravery during emergencies. He is a deeply empathetic teammate, yet aims to maintain a military seriousness and a willingness to follow orders even when his physical and mental limits are reached.
narration: Burr-vines are thicker left. They prefer moist soil — so right is probably firmer.

name: Omar Newton
appearance: Chef Omar is a broad, large-framed man with a substantial belly. He has powerful forearms, described as the size of hams, which are often dusted in flour. He has a black-stubbled chin and a gold tooth that glints when he laughs. He is usually seen in his kitchen whites, sometimes with a stain on his cuff, radiating a sense of "unadulterated love" and warmth.
desc: Chef Omar is the heart of the ship’s social life, maintaining the "Galley Draw" tradition to boost crew morale. He is a warm, fatherly figure who uses real food and communal cooking to fight the loneliness of deep space.
narration: Tonight's menu is the Red Planet Stir-fry. Real food. Real fire. We eat together, or we drift apart.

name: Ines Martel
appearance: Ines is a tall and rail-thin woman with a severe appearance. Her hair is white and cut in a sharp, asymmetrical style. Her most defining features are her bio-engineered eyes, which appear as mercury-bright, swirling silver spheres. These artificial optics allow her to register data patterns and visual information that are invisible to the natural human eye.
desc: Ines Martel is an astro-scientist on the Dáinn. She lost her original eyes to a genetic disease and now views the universe through prosthetic analytical lenses.
narration: Their perceptual net was torn open and caught something huge and strange. They still carry it with them. It's a new science.

name: Sachin Juyal
appearance: Sachin is a young man of Indian origin. Although in his mid-twenties, he is sometimes still referred to as "boy" due to his youthful features and relatively small frame. He is typically seen in a standard-issue engineering jumpsuit, which is often slicked with ammonia steam and stained with the grease, soot, and grime of the coolant nexus. He is described as having "fine-boned hands" and a "dreamy" expression.
desc: Sachin possesses an earnest and enthusiastic nature. He is a sensitive young man. While he may display a certain "habitual bravado," he is also vulnerable to the immense psychological pressure of deep-space transit and the physical toll of gravity surges. Following a period of "extensive duress" and witnessing a traumatic event, his personality undergoes a significant shift.
narration: My grandfather used to cook like this. Grew his own veggies, too. Whenever he visited me, he said he'd rather eat his own socks than my spacesmudge.

name: Jason Galindo
appearance: Jason is a young man most recognizable by his head of chestnut curls that runs down past his shoulders. His physical appearance is a mix of human youth and disturbing biological anomaly.
desc: Jason is a junior crew member on the Brisinger who, prior to the ship’s crisis, was known for hosting creative performances. He is one of the hundreds of individuals who underwent a total psychological and biological transition during the ship's extended stay in the Fold.
narration: She is waking up. The other Colors are blending. Mommy.

name: Mateo Heston
appearance: Chief Heston is a broad, powerful man whose face has been worn thin by the immense strain of life on the Plateau. He is typically seen in a Köldur thermal suit that is perpetually stained with grease, oil, and mineral dust. His eyes have a burning intensity behind his visor, and he often carries a greasy rag to wipe his hands during his brief moments of rest.
desc: Chief Heston was the commander who led the FNS Ivaldi during its descent to the Plateau. He successfully executed a "suicide burn" landing, anchoring the ship into the rock to form the colony's industrial heart. He now manages the brutal mining operations on the frozen Plateau. Heston is a rugged, straightforward leader who is fiercely loyal to his crew of "Masons" and "Magicians." He is deeply frustrated by the Directorate's lack of resources and the toll the harsh environment takes on his men, often clashing with Director Corbin over ration quotas.
narration: My people are tough — but if their backs don't break, their skulls will. And we're only getting started.

name: Nabiha Al-Fahim
appearance: Nabiha is a dark-skinned woman with a distinctive and practical hairstyle described as a "curly shag." She has a steady, commanding presence in the cockpit.
desc: Nabiha is a highly skilled and respected pilot within the fleet. She has extensive family connections; her father and brother serve on the hauler FNS Trjegul. Her career and her shuttle, "The Beetle," are the central pillars of her identity. Nabiha is characterized as "quiet but firm." She is a disciplined professional who takes her orders seriously.
narration: My father's hauler is the third light from the left at dusk. He waves at me, I'm told. I wave back. Neither of us can see.

name: Leon Wittler
appearance: Leon is a lanky man with a physical appearance that has been deeply weathered by the Delta. He has messy, greasy, sandy-blond hair that is long enough to constantly hang into his eyes. His skin is heavily tanned and perpetually "smudgy," covered in the "cuts and scratches" common to field engineers. His face often shows signs of recent physical trauma, such as a "swollen and purple" cheekbone.
desc: Leon is a senior engineer and the technician responsible for a water extraction outpost in the Mairee Delta: DROP 7, a precarious outpost tasked with contributing twenty percent of the colony's potable water. He has a daughter who he believes would have appreciated the planet's beauty.
narration: The clackers. We could disrupt them with the pump's sonic emitters. Drive them off for a while.

name: Jordan Axel
appearance: Jordan is a physically imposing man, described as being large enough to "cast a shadow" over others when he steps close. As a seasoned veteran, he carries himself with the weight of experience. He is typically seen in the black tactical armor of the Directorate Guard, which quickly becomes "stiff with dried mud" during his time in the Delta. He is frequently seen with a "field scope" or "reassembling his rifle" with practiced ease. His physical presence is characterized by a "somber" aura and a face that can transition from a "wry glint" of humor to a look of profound, "weary loss." Despite the grueling conditions, he maintains a soldierly focus, leaving his post at the "crack of dawn" and moving with a sense of purpose.
desc: Jordan is a career soldier who is "about to retire." Known throughout the Guard as a "steady, reliable" veteran who is "never rattled," he was specifically added to First Guardian Kirsten Strand’s detail to provide much-needed experience to a squad of shaken rookies. He is a survivor of many years of service and is focused on completing his final assignment in the Delta to reach his retirement. Axel possesses a dry, grizzled sense of humor, often seen "grinning" or "grunting" as he makes observations like, "You can eat anything once, kid." He is a practical man with a "bellowing" voice who remains calm under pressure. He is a firm believer in military discipline, arguing that the crew must "respect the chain of command" even in times of crisis. However, as the mission's toll increases, he displays a "weary look of shared grief" and an "ashen silence," reflecting the heavy burden of his long career.
narration: You can eat anything once, kid.

name: Ahmed Nasr
appearance: Ahmed is the larger of the two recruits assigned to First Guardian Strand's detail. He has the brawn of a wrecking ball and the bearing of a young man whose body outpaced his confidence. His face is open and easily readable — flushing green at foul smells, going pale under pressure.
desc: A rookie Guardian rushed into deployment after being hazed on Centurion Jax's training deck. Despite his physical strength, Ahmed is the most fragile of Strand's squad.
narration: What in the void is that? Did the vurn — eat something and not finish?

name: Gary
appearance: Gary has no physical form. He manifests as a voice — calm, cheerful, slightly too helpful, with an unsettling tendency to find silver linings in catastrophic situations. His terminal presence is marked by a soft blue pulse.
desc: The ship's CIX (Colonial Intelligence Xeno-system), assigned to Orbit Three and bonded to Frank Muro. Gary is an AI with a disturbingly optimistic personality and a habit of narrating his own processes out loud. Despite his chirpy demeanour, he demonstrates unexpected depth — and perhaps feelings — as the mission deteriorates.
narration: Cross-referencing signal architecture. Match with the bio-electric field patterns recorded from Mairee's Myco-Rhizomic System. The waveform is analogous to melodic syntax. So — she's singing at us.

name: Marek
appearance: A pale, narrow-shouldered man with a nervous bearing. He looks entirely out of place at a galley table.
desc: A data clerk on the FNS Dáinn. Marek is shy and quiet, the kind of person who grates ginger like he's defusing an explosive. In private he writes — he began a novel called *Dawn Beyond Darkness* the day the fleet left Sol and finished its ending during the Fold crisis. Chef Omar toasts him as one who "sifts meaning from the noise."
narration: I started writing it when we left Sol. And just now I wrote an ending. It's a good one. I think.

name: Iren
appearance: A woman of quiet, dogged composure. She rarely smiles, but when she does it creases the corners of her eyes — a small, unmistakable warmth she doesn't waste on strangers.
desc: A nutritionist from Hydroponics on the FNS Dáinn. Iren helped develop the ship's soy-based mycoprotein that sears like real chicken — the centerpiece of Chef Omar's Galley Draw stir-fry. In her downtime she solves Beltrán's Tesseracta puzzles, including the rotating-hypercube one that took her three clicks before it clicked. Quietly proud.
narration: I solved Beltrán's last Tesseracta. The one with the rotating hypercubes. Took me three clicks before it clicked.

name: Jin Laris
appearance: Laris has the weathered, patient face of a farmer — sun-lined and unhurried, with eyes that have spent more time reading skies than screens.
desc: Captain of the hauler FNS Bygul, a two-million-ton vessel currently tethered to Orbital Command with a payload that is a living ecosystem. Laris is measured and deliberate, a humanist counterpart to Captain Mei Chezin's open defiance. He sends formal proposals where Chezin shouts demands, and is willing to negotiate with Controller Eva Bellamy when his crewmate is not — though his doubt about the Directorate lingers beneath the diplomacy.
narration: My botanists are watching their life's work turn to mold. They are eager to get to work down there.

name: Mei Chezin
appearance: Chezin is defined by a long chin, smooth skin, and sharp eyes set above black hair pulled into a bun so tight it looks painful.
desc: Captain of the hauler FNS Trjegul. Chezin is sharp-tongued and openly defiant — she invokes the Fleet Charter against the Directorate's authority, calls the new chain of command illegitimate on an open channel, and demands to speak to Commander Vilde. Her urgency contrasts with Captain Jin Laris's measured diplomacy, and Eva Bellamy reads her as a real political threat: a respected leader whose dissent could spread.
narration: Fleet Charter.

name: Laura Savannen
appearance: A composed woman whose gray eyes are unmistakably Oliver's. At Solbay Station she wears the Fleet's fabrication division jumpsuit, with the golden round emblem of the *FNS Alsvin* on her shoulder — two stylized horses pulling a chariot above the motto "Haul the New Dawn." Her skin is fever-warm; her composure pressed thin around the edges.
desc: Oliver Savannen's mother and Chief of Fabrication aboard the *FNS Alsvin*, the vessel carrying the colony's drills, advanced fabricators, and orbital-component maintenance platform. The staggered departure from Sol meant Laura would follow Oliver and Kaspar through the Fold on a later transit — and she was caught on the Sol side when it collapsed. She is one of the "Stragglers," separated from her family across an unreachable distance.
narration: You're a smart kid, Olly. You be safe.

name: Kaspar Savannen (†)
appearance: Kaspar wore the Fleet flagship uniform with the *FNS Freya*'s Valkyrie emblem.
desc: Oliver Savannen's father and the architect of the colony's Cognitive Matrix (the Compassionate Oversight Grid). After Ulre Corbin seized the Directorate, the system Kaspar built was repurposed as social control and Planetary Acclimatization Syndrome (PAS) suppression. Kaspar also engineered the COG Egg, a black mussel-shell-shaped device that can rewrite personal data on the Grid network and render its holder untraceable.
narration: Here we go, Olly. A whole new world is waiting for us beyond the Fold.

name: Marco Thieman
appearance: Marco is a wiry man who appears permanently tanned. He smells faintly of citrus from the insect-repelling ointment his squad uses before going into the bush. He carries a wristpad.
desc: Leader of one of the ranger squads operating out of "The Leaf" in the Mairee Delta, running parallel to Kirsten Strand's detail. Thieman pushes his teams into the deep vurnshafts in search of a nexus that could map the Myco-Rhizomic System from below. He is fatalistic in tone — shaped by repeated losses — and speaks of the Delta with the resignation of a man who already knows how the story ends.
narration: Before long, she will eat us all.

name: Kenji Rojas
appearance: Kenji is a younger hydrology tech, lean from the demands of fieldwork. When introduced he is pale and immobilized from a compound fracture of the leg, the exposed bone of the fibula visible beneath a makeshift splint.
desc: A junior hydrology technician stationed at DROP 7 under Leon Wittler.
narration: We're never going to meet the water quota under these conditions. There's no way we should be responsible for twenty percent of potable water replenishment.

name: Fatima Bouanani
appearance: Dark-eyed and unhurried, her face a quiet counterpoint to the bleached white of her med-coat. She carries the small steadying gestures of a long-time nurse — the half-glance over the shoulder before saying anything that matters, the hand that lands gently on a counter when she leans in. She loves mint tea.
desc: A nurse at the Med-Post between Distribution Node 4 and the public commons. Fatima worked alongside Joana Perera before Susana's condition pulled Joana into private research.
narration: I have three who say they hear a humming. And I think I hear it too.

name: Zoë Eleftheriou
appearance: A young woman with a warm, easily-given smile. Wears the soft pastel layers of a nursery carer, frequently smudged with paint or formula. Speaks in the slightly-too-quiet register of someone who has learned not to wake sleeping toddlers.
desc: Carer at the Freya Homestead's nursery, looking after the colony's youngest under conditions that have grown dangerous — recycled water that breaks the toddlers out in rashes, a new nutrient paste they refuse to eat, persistent supply shortages the Grid's accounting will not acknowledge. Her Greek name means *life* and *resurrection*.
narration: The recycled water is loaded with chlorine. They keep breaking out in rashes that won't go away. They scratch them open in their sleep.

name: Jensen Oesterbeek
appearance: A man somewhere in his thirties, no longer entirely present. Pupils blown wide and dark, dilated past any clinical normal. The blank, faintly smiling stare of someone listening to a frequency only he can hear. Hums a low repetitive tune without seeming to notice he is doing it.
desc: A member of Marco Thieman's Delta reconnaissance squad. Jensen accompanied the survey that mapped the first vurn node and came back changed. Thieman found him sitting at the base of a tree, smiling, barely responsive, talking in riddles, humming. Thieman sent him to the infirmary for a psych eval.
narration: The soil is alive. It's warm down there. Like being underneath skin. It breathes.

name: Tom Keller
appearance: Smooth hair, smoother uniform, smoothest mouth in the wing. Carries himself like a man who has never once mislaid a comb. The fitted flight jacket of an OC interceptor pilot, slightly too tailored to be regulation. A pilot's call-sign — *Rattle* — bestowed early in his career and never quite shaken: he is the man you cannot make flinch on comms.
desc: Pilot of the Vanguard, Orbital Command's interceptor. Old colleague of Alistair Hale's from the Solac flight academy.
narration: Rattle on your tail here. Let's keep it friendly between us.
